understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete the level by getting the selectable piece(s) onto the goal tile(s) marked with '+', with the win condition requiring every '+' to be occupied by a selectable piece at the same time before the step budget ran out.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: A single piece was "selected" at a time and all actions applied to that selected piece. CLICK on a grid coordinate attempted to move the selected piece directly to the clicked tile (it behaved like a jump/teleport only if the destination was valid/reachable under the level’s constraints); if the destination was invalid (e.g., a wall/obstacle tile, outside the playable area, or otherwise not a legal landing spot), the piece did not move. MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN attempted to move the selected piece one tile in that direction; if blocked by an obstacle/wall/boundary, it did not move. I did not observe any successful pushing interactions in this run (attempted moves into blocked spaces simply resulted in no movement), so either pushing was not supported in this level/state or I never lined up a pushable object correctly. Selection itself appeared implicit/unchanged throughout (clicks moved the currently selected piece rather than selecting a different one).

orient_history: Movement is grid-based in 3-pixel (1-cell) steps; pieces slide one cell per MOVE_* unless blocked by walls/other collidables. Likely need to switch selection via CLICK to move the other selectable if possible, and ultimately occupy the goal with a selectable.

Hypothesis: movement is grid-based in 3-pixel (1-cell) steps but walls fully enclose the current piece so MOVE actions cannot work here. CLICK allows relocating a selectable to a clicked position (likely snapping to grid / nearest valid empty), enabling placement onto goal tiles. To win, a selectable must occupy a goal tile area; with one goal region, we just need one selectable on any + cell. The goal area appears reachable only via CLICK since there are no corridors.

Hypothesis: CLICK attempts to teleport/move the selected piece to the clicked pixel position if the destination area is empty and reachable/valid; otherwise nothing happens. Since normal movement is fully blocked, we must use CLICK to place pieces onto the goal tiles. Win likely requires both selectables to occupy goal tiles (or at least cover all goal cells) simultaneously; with only one goal region, probably need to place a piece onto it (and possibly both pieces if multiple goal tiles are required). Next, try clicking directly on the goal's top-left pixel position to snap P onto the goal area.

Movement actions attempt to shift by one piece-width/one cell-step but are currently impossible because the selected piece is enclosed by walls. Likely need to select the other piece (S) and move it toward the goal instead.

Hypothesis: movement via arrow actions is impossible while surrounded; progress requires CLICK-based teleportation/switching between duplicated selectables. Likely need to click on the other selectable (or a valid empty cell) to relocate the selected piece into the open goal chamber; clicks into walls/invalid regions do nothing.

Discarding prior hypothesis. New hypothesis: CLICK only swaps/teleports between pre-defined selectable anchor positions, and MOVE_* attempts to shift the currently selected piece by one cell but can be blocked. Since P is boxed in by walls, moving is impossible unless we first switch selection to the other selectable (which may have mobility) using a non-CLICK method; if selection switching is not possible, then MOVE_* will confirm immobility and we must revisit.

CLICK moves the currently selected piece to the clicked location only if there is a clear, valid placement region for the full 3x3 footprint (not overlapping walls). Movement actions shift by one cell (3 pixels) but require free space; currently the selected piece is fully boxed in, so we likely need to select the other piece and try clicking it into the goal area.

Only observed effective mechanic is CLICK teleporting the selected piece to the position of another selectable (swap/teleport between identical selectables). Direct MOVE is impossible because both selectables are boxed in by walls. To win, likely need to CLICK on the goal tile itself (or within goal area) to teleport there if allowed, or CLICK the goal to activate/convert it. Previous clicks at [35,17] and [36,18] did nothing, so goal may require clicking a specific cell within the + cluster or clicking while a different selectable is selected.

Movement is in 3-pixel (1 cell) increments but blocked by walls; since both pieces are trapped, the only progress possible is via CLICK interactions between selectables until one can be positioned onto the goal region (or selection must be changed to move the other piece if it has different mobility).

history.summary: Level 1 step budget exhausted.
Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete the level by getting the selectable piece(s) onto the goal tile(s) marked with '+', with the win condition requiring every '+' to be occupied by a selectable piece at the same time before the step budget ran out.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: A single piece was "selected" at a time and all actions applied to that selected piece. CLICK on a grid coordinate attempted to move the selected piece directly to the clicked tile (it behaved like a jump/teleport only if the destination was valid/reachable under the level’s constraints); if the destination was invalid (e.g., a wall/obstacle tile, outside the playable area, or otherwise not a legal landing spot), the piece did not move. MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN attempted to move the selected piece one tile in that direction; if blocked by an obstacle/wall/boundary, it did not move. I did not observe any successful pushing interactions in this run (attempted moves into blocked spaces simply resulted in no movement), so either pushing was not supported in this level/state or I never lined up a pushable object correctly. Selection itself appeared implicit/unchanged throughout (clicks moved the currently selected piece rather than selecting a different one).
